author: uppinote20
description: Sync notes bidirectionally between your vault and Airtable, SeaTable, or Supabase databases.

manifest.author: uppinote
manifest.description: Sync notes bidirectionally between your vault and Airtable, SeaTable, or Supabase databases.
manifest.name: Auto Note Importer

name: Auto Note Importer

releases.author: claude[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- Prevent frontmatter edits from overwriting structured remote fields (#108)
  - Introduces `isPushable` to the `FieldTypeMapper` interface, stricter than `!isReadOnly`, to exclude object-shaped fields (Airtable `barcode`/collaborators, SeaTable `collaborator`/`geolocation`, Supabase `json`/`jsonb`) from push payloads
  - Fixes a data-loss bug where a jsonb column with an empty frontmatter placeholder was overwritten with `null` on push
  - Fail-closed for unknown types — unrecognized field types are treated as not pushable

### Performance
- Cache verification result on credential form to skip redundant `verifySetup` probe on Save (#112)
  - A successful connection test sets a 60-second freshness fingerprint, allowing the next Save to skip the `verifySetup` network probe
  - Fingerprint is invalidated on any field edit, probe failure, or `needsSetup` — a changed or bad credential can never ride a stale "verified" result past the save gate
  - Primarily affects Supabase (the only provider that implements `verifySetup`)

### Refactoring
- Unify exhaustiveness guards behind a shared `assertNever` helper (#113)
  - Adds `src/utils/assertNever` — enforces switch/if exhaustiveness at compile time (missing cases after union extension fail to compile) and throws at runtime
  - Routes all five throwing exhaustiveness guards (sync mode/scope, conflict mode, setup-requirement kind, credential type) through the helper
  - Upgrades `migration.ts`'s guard to a real `satisfies never` check that catches missing cases at compile time

### Testing
- Add cross-provider `FieldTypeMapper` parity contract tests (#111)
  - Registry-driven test suite that validates all registered mappers satisfy shared invariants: `filenameSafe ⊆ subfolderSafe`, no subfolder-safe type maps to `attachment`/`link`/`unknown`, and prototype-chain names fail closed
  - Automatically covers future providers the moment they register a mapper
  - Sorts `FILENAME_SAFE_TYPES` in Airtable and SeaTable mappers for deterministic test output

### Maintenance
- Bump CI artifact and attest actions to Node 24 runtimes (#106)
  - `actions/upload-artifact` v4.6.2 → v7.0.1 (Node 24)
  - `actions/download-artifact` v4.3.0 → v8.0.1 (Node 24)
  - `actions/attest-build-provenance` v2.4.0 → v4.1.0 (Node 24)
  - Preemptive update ahead of GitHub's Node 20 runner removal on 2026-09-16
- Upgrade TypeScript 4.7.4 → 5.9.3 and `@typescript-eslint` v5 → v8 (#109)
  - Updates ESLint toolchain to `@typescript-eslint` v8 (ESLint 8 retained, no config churn)
  - Replaces `const _exhaustive: never` workaround with idiomatic `satisfies never` (TS 4.9+)
  - No runtime changes — compiler-only upgrade, emitted JS is identical

releases.author: claude[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- Fix SeaTable metadata cache robustness and migrate from native `fetch` to Obsidian `requestUrl` (#104)
  - Recover response shape when older Obsidian builds reject on 4xx/5xx (mirrors `SupabaseMetadataCache`)
  - Fix `dtable_server` gateway fallback to match `SeaTableClient` so settings UI no longer 404s
  - Swallow `SyntaxError` from non-JSON proxy responses, surfacing them as friendly `HTTP {status}` errors
  - Widen status check to full 2xx range, add `Accept: application/json` header, and fix race condition in `clearForCred` during in-flight requests
- Fix CSS compatibility for Obsidian 1.4.5: replace `text-decoration-color` with `border-bottom`, remove `!important`, and raise `.ani-field-error` selector specificity (#104)
- Fix SeaTable sync failures behind corporate proxies by accepting the full 2xx status range in all client methods (#105)
  - `getBaseToken`, `fetchNotes`, `fetchRecord`, and `batchUpdate` now treat any 2xx response as success
  - Mirrors the behavior already present in `SeaTableMetadataCache`

### Maintenance
- Harden release workflow with two-job split (`build` / `release`) and SLSA build-provenance attestation via `actions/attest-build-provenance` (#104)
  - Postinstall scripts can no longer run in the same job as the OIDC signing identity
  - Pin attestation action to SHA instead of mutable tag; include `manifest.json` as attestation subject
- Update `manifest.json` `authorUrl` to profile URL and drop `builtin-modules` external dependency (#104)
- Correct README "Permissions & Disclosures" section to match vault APIs actually used (#104)
- Resolve 3 pre-existing ESLint warnings: narrow `as any` cast in `main.ts` and drop non-null assertions in `field-cache.ts` (#105)

releases.author: claude[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### New Features
- Add per-config subfolder slash literal toggle (#97)
  - New `subfolderTreatSlashAsLiteral` setting per config: when enabled, slash characters in subfolder field values are treated as literal (e.g. `AC/DC` → single folder `AC-DC`) instead of creating nested folders
  - Default is `false`, preserving legacy behavior where `2024/Q1` creates a two-level folder hierarchy
  - Toggle appears beneath every subfolder field picker across Airtable, SeaTable, and Supabase configurations
  - Existing settings migrate automatically with `false` fallback — no behavior change for current users
- Add `isSubfolderSafe` field filter and UI parity for subfolder dropdowns across all providers (#100)
  - New `isSubfolderSafe` / `getSubfolderSafeTypes` methods on `FieldTypeMapper` exclude attachment, link, and object-shaped column types (collaborator, geolocation, barcode, etc.) from subfolder field pickers — fixes over-restrictive filtering for Airtable and under-restrictive filtering for SeaTable / Supabase
  - Settings UI now surfaces stale stored subfolder/filename values that no longer match the active filter as a synthetic `(unsupported / hidden)` option, so users can re-select without silent data loss
  - Runtime guard in `determineFolderPath` falls back to flat layout when a field value resolves to `[object Object]`, protecting legacy configs that predate this change

### Maintenance
- Fix release workflow trigger + bump GitHub Actions to v6 + add repo templates (#99)
  - `release.yml` tag filter changed from `"*"` to `"[0-9]*"` to prevent sync tags from accidentally triggering the publish workflow
  - `actions/checkout` and `actions/setup-node` updated from v3/v4 → v6; Node.js bumped from 18 → 20 (active LTS) across all workflows
  - Added `CODEOWNERS`, `pull_request_template.md`, and issue templates (bug report, feature request)

releases.author: claude[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### New Features
- Add Supabase / PostgreSQL provider with full read, write, and bidirectional sync (#90)
  - Connects via PostgREST (direct `requestUrl`, no `@supabase/supabase-js` dependency)
  - OpenAPI introspection populates Schema/Table/View/Filename/Subfolder dropdowns in the settings UI, matching the SeaTable UX pattern
  - Supports legacy `anon`/`service_role` JWTs and new `sb_publishable_…`/`sb_secret_…` keys; the credential form auto-detects the key kind and surfaces an RLS-impact hint
  - Primary key is auto-detected from the OpenAPI spec and editable for views or composite-PK cases

### Bug Fixes
- Show inline RPC setup banner in the Supabase credential form when the required database function is missing (#92)
  - Replaces the transient toast notice with a persistent inline banner that stays visible until the setup SQL is applied and verified
  - Blocks credential save when a publishable key is used against a project missing the `ani_supabase_schema` RPC, preventing silent misconfiguration
  - Save and Test buttons show loading state during the probe; changing any credential field auto-clears a stale banner

### Documentation
- Refresh README and CLAUDE.md for Supabase provider and inline RPC banner (#94)
  - Adds Supabase to the provider list and connection card flow in README
  - Corrects publishable-key section: banner location, button text (Verify), and save-blocked-until-verified guarantee
  - Adds `SupabaseClient` to the Key Classes reference in CLAUDE.md

releases.author: claude[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### New Features
- Add metadata-driven dropdowns for SeaTable connection settings (#81)
  - Table, View, Filename field, and Subfolder field selectors now populate automatically from the SeaTable API instead of requiring manual text entry
  - New `SeaTableMetadataCache` service fetches and caches tables/columns/views per credential, so the settings tab loads metadata once and reuses it across re-renders
  - Gracefully falls back to text inputs when the API token is empty or metadata fetch fails, and auto-upgrades to dropdowns on the next render once the token is reachable

### Bug Fixes
- Fix connection card not re-rendering when switching credential type in the dropdown (#80)
  - Credential type changes (e.g. SeaTable → Airtable) now correctly rebuild the provider instead of calling `reconfigure()` on a mismatched type, which previously threw and silently prevented the card from updating
  - Connection card now renders based on `credential.type` alone; missing secrets show a `Setup required` badge instead of dropping the card entirely
  - New e2e test case covers bidirectional credential-type switching to prevent regression

### Refactoring
- Align `batchUpdate` batch-limit guard with `SyncResult` failure contract (#78)
  - Size-limit guard now returns `SyncResult[]` failures instead of throwing, matching all other failure paths
  - Extracted `buildBatchFailures` and `BATCH_LIMIT_ERROR` helpers to `api-errors.ts`, shared across both Airtable and SeaTable clients

### Documentation
- Refresh README and CLAUDE.md for 0.9.0 multi-provider release (#77)
  - Updated README to cover both Airtable and SeaTable credentials, setup, commands, field types, and troubleshooting
  - Expanded CLAUDE.md architecture overview to reflect the full 0.9.0 module structure including new providers, types, and services
  - Corrected conflict resolution mode label from `airtable-wins` to `remote-wins` in docs
  - Added e2e test commands and code ↔ test bidirectional link conventions to CLAUDE.md

### Testing
- Extract shared e2e harness helpers into a common module (#79)
  - New `tests/e2e/obsidian-helpers.mjs` consolidates ~250 LOC duplicated across all four E2E harnesses
  - Exports `buildSyncHarnessHelpers`, `buildSettingsHarnessHelpers`, `makeSetConfigAndQuery`, and `buildConfigExpr` factories
  - Reduces 4-way duplication to a single definition; future provider harnesses reuse helpers with ~80 LOC savings each

releases.author: claude[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### New Features
- Add SeaTable as a supported database provider (#72)
  - New `SeaTableClient` implements `DatabaseProvider` with Base-Token caching, system field stripping, and batch update support for all writable column types
  - New `SeaTableFieldMapper` maps SeaTable native column types (`text`, `long-text`, `single-select`, `formula`, `link-formula`, `ctime`, …) to the standard field type taxonomy
  - Settings UI renders a SeaTable-specific connection card (Table ID / View ID / Filename field / Subfolder field) when a SeaTable credential is active
  - To connect: create a Read & Write API Token in your SeaTable Base → Advanced settings → API Tokens, then add it as a SeaTable credential in plugin settings
- Add provider-aware credential form with connection test button (#61)
  - New `CredentialFormRenderer` interface enables provider-specific credential UI without hardcoding Airtable logic
  - Settings UI now shows a **Test Connection** button that validates API credentials against the provider's API
  - Edit mode pre-populates existing credential fields for a smoother editing experience
- Include provider label in bidirectional command names (#68)
  - Bidirectional sync commands now show `with {Provider}` (e.g. "Bidirectional sync current note with Airtable") for consistency with pull (`from`) and push (`to`) commands
  - Command IDs are unchanged — existing hotkey bindings are preserved

### Refactoring
- Introduce `DatabaseProvider` abstraction to prepare for multi-database support (#58)
  - New `DatabaseProvider` interface decouples sync logic from Airtable-specific implementation
  - New `ProviderRegistry` factory maps credential types to provider instances
  - Credentials are now a discriminated union supporting `airtable`, `seatable`, `supabase`, `notion`, and `custom-api` types
  - Settings UI shows a provider type dropdown; non-Airtable types display "Not yet supported" until implemented in upcoming releases
  - Fully backward compatible — no data migration required for existing Airtable credentials
- Extract provider-agnostic field type system (#60)
  - New `FieldTypeMapper` interface and `StandardFieldType` taxonomy replace hard-coded Airtable field type constants
  - New `AirtableFieldMapper` maps 29 Airtable field types to standard types (text, number, computed, system, etc.)
  - `ProviderRegistry` extended with field type mapper lookup, enabling Settings UI to query type info without a provider instance
  - Fully backward compatible — no behavior change for existing Airtable usage
- Rename sync mode values and command IDs to provider-agnostic names (#62)
  - `SyncMode` literals renamed from `'to-airtable'`/`'from-airtable'` to `'push'`/`'pull'`; `'bidirectional'` unchanged
  - Command IDs renamed: `sync-from-airtable-*` → `sync-pull-*`, `sync-*-to-airtable-*` → `sync-push-*-*`
  - Command names and status bar messages now derive the provider label dynamically (e.g. "Syncing from SeaTable…" for SeaTable configs)
  - **Breaking:** existing custom hotkeys bound to old command IDs must be re-bound to the new `sync-pull-*` / `sync-push-*` IDs
- Use provider-reported batch size for push operations (#69)
  - Sync orchestrator now reads `provider.capabilities.batchUpdateMaxSize` at runtime instead of importing a hardcoded Airtable constant
  - Future providers with different batch limits will automatically use the correct batch size without code changes
- Rename settings fields and conflict mode to provider-agnostic names, settings v2→v3 migration (#70)
  - `autoSyncFormulas` setting renamed to `autoSyncComputedFields`; conflict resolution value `'airtable-wins'` renamed to `'remote-wins'`
  - Settings UI labels updated (e.g. "Auto-sync server-computed fields", "Remote wins (overwrite Obsidian)")
  - Automatic v2→v3 migration runs on plugin load — existing settings are preserved with no manual action required
- Harden settings migration and reduce credential lookup overhead (#71)
  - Migration now safely handles malformed credentials (missing `id`, unknown `type`, non-object entries) and non-array fields without crashing
  - Future-version settings are refused rather than partially applied, preventing data corruption on downgrade
  - Credential lookups during settings save reduced from O(N×M) to O(N+M) via a pre-built index

### Maintenance
- Add exhaustive switch guard to `processSyncRequest` (#67)
  - Adding a new `SyncMode` value without handling it in the mode-dispatch switch now causes a compile-time error, preventing silent fall-through

releases.author: claude[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- Fix missing `styles.css` in release assets (#57)
  - Custom styles were not applied for users who installed via GitHub release
  - Added `styles.css` to both `gh release upload` and `gh release create` steps in the release workflow

releases.author: claude[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### New Features
- Add multi-base/table configuration support (#48)
  - Manage multiple Airtable bases and tables simultaneously, each with independent sync settings
  - New tab-based Settings UI for switching between, adding, and removing configurations
  - Credential registry to name and reuse API keys across configurations; supports per-credential rate limiting
  - Per-config commands dynamically registered (e.g., "Sync from Airtable — Blog Posts")
  - Folder overlap validation to prevent configurations from targeting the same or nested paths
  - Automatic migration from v1 single-config settings to the new v2 multi-config format

releases.author: claude[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### New Features
- Add Airtable view filtering support (#45)
  - Choose a specific Airtable view from the Settings UI to sync only the records that view exposes (filtered/sorted)
  - View selector dropdown appears after a table is selected; defaults to all records when left empty
  - Changing the base or table automatically resets the selected view
  - Fields and views for a table are now fetched in a single API call and cached together

### Bug Fixes
- Fix memory leak in settings tab when closing the settings panel (#46)
  - Debounce timer is now properly cleared when the settings tab is hidden
  - Enabled TypeScript `strict: true` for stronger compile-time type safety

### Other Changes
- Add comprehensive tests for sync pipeline modules (#47)
  - 56 new tests covering SyncOrchestrator, AirtableClient, FileWatcher, and FieldCache
  - Test suite expanded from 267 to 323 tests
  - Extended Obsidian mock infrastructure with app, file, and folder factory helpers

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New

### Auto-generate Bases database files (#43)
The plugin can now generate `.airtable-bases` files containing base and table metadata after syncing from Airtable, enabling Obsidian Bases table/card views. Disabled by default — enable in settings with file location options (vault root / synced folder / custom path).

### Retry on transient network errors (#44)
The rate limiter now retries on transient network errors (DNS failures, timeouts, connection resets) with exponential backoff (1s → 2s → 4s). Uses an allowlist approach — only known network error patterns are retried, while programming errors fail fast.

## Improvements
- `isTransientError` uses allowlist (`ECONNRESET`, `ETIMEDOUT`, `ENOTFOUND`, `net::ERR_*`, etc.) instead of retrying all unknown errors
- Retry budget is shared between 429 responses and network errors
- Updated JSDoc across `RateLimiter` class, `execute()` method, and related constants

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/uppinote20/obsidian-auto-note-importer/compare/0.5.4...0.6.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### New Features
- **Respect Retry-After header on 429 responses** (#28): RateLimiter now automatically detects 429 (Too Many Requests) responses and retries after the server-specified delay
  - Duck-type response detection — no dependency on Obsidian types
  - Case-insensitive `Retry-After` header parsing (seconds format)
  - Configurable max retries (default: 3) with 30s fallback delay
  - All AirtableClient methods benefit automatically — no caller changes needed

### Tests
- 8 new test cases covering retry behavior, header parsing edge cases, and non-retryable response passthrough

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/uppinote20/obsidian-auto-note-importer/compare/0.5.3...0.5.4

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- **Surface error details in debug mode** (#37): Show error message in Notice when `debugMode` is enabled in `createNoteFromRemote` catch block

### Refactoring
- **Eliminate redundant file lookup** (#36): Skip `getFilesToSync()` call in `from-airtable` mode by restructuring `processSyncRequest`
- **Extract SyncOrchestrator** (#35): Separate sync orchestration logic from `main.ts` into dedicated `SyncOrchestrator` class

### Code Quality
- **Fix lint warnings** (#38): Remove non-null assertions, use `Setting.setHeading()` per Obsidian UI guidelines
- Add `@handbook` markers for bidirectional code-docs linking

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/uppinote20/obsidian-auto-note-importer/compare/0.5.2...0.5.3

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **#29**: Prevent `pendingFiles` loss on sync failure — moved `clear()` to success path so files can be retried on next cycle
- **#30**: Filter `.` and `..` segments in `sanitizeFolderPath` to block path traversal attacks via Airtable subfolder field
- **#31**: Replace `NodeJS.Timeout` with `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>` for browser-compatible timer type in Electron renderer

## Tests

- Added 3 test cases for path traversal filtering in `sanitizeFolderPath` (44/44 passed)
- E2E 11/11 passed

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes

- Add refresh buttons to base, table, and field dropdowns in settings
- Each button clears only its level of cache (with cascading invalidation for dependent data)
- No plugin restart needed to reflect Airtable schema changes

## Details

- `FieldCache`: Added `clearBases()`, `clearTables(baseId)`, `clearFields(baseId, tableId)` methods with hierarchical cache invalidation
- `SettingsTab`: Added `addExtraButton` with refresh icon to each Airtable metadata dropdown
- Deduplicated cache-clearing logic and extracted `configureRefreshButton` helper

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/uppinote20/obsidian-auto-note-importer/compare/0.5.0...0.5.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

Major feature addition: bidirectional sync between Airtable and Obsidian, along with a full architectural refactoring to a domain-driven modular structure.

## New Features

- **Bidirectional sync** — Push note edits from Obsidian to Airtable, pull changes back
- **Conflict resolution** — Three modes: `obsidian-wins`, `airtable-wins`, `manual`
- **File watching** — Auto-detect file changes in the sync folder (configurable debounce)
- **Formula/relation auto-sync** — Pull back Airtable formula recomputed values after push
- **Debug mode** — Delay multiplier for development and debugging
- **New commands** — Push, pull, and bidirectional sync by scope (current / modified / all)

## Architecture

- Monolithic → domain-based modular architecture (`services/`, `core/`, `builders/`, `file-operations/`, `ui/`, `utils/`)
- `SyncQueue` — Prevents concurrent syncs, merges duplicate requests
- `ConflictResolver` — Strategy-pattern conflict resolution
- `FileWatcher` — Debounce-based file change detection
- `RateLimiter` — API request throttling
- `FieldCache` — Airtable field metadata caching

## Bug Fixes

- Resolve FileWatcher `isSyncing` race condition (split into external/internal flags)
- Add Airtable record ID format validation before API calls
- Fix local-only fields being pushed to Airtable (422 errors)
- Strengthen read-only field filtering (formula, rollup, lookup)

## Testing

- 10 Vitest unit test suites / 220 tests
- 11 E2E test scenarios via Chrome DevTools Protocol
- One-command E2E runner (`npm run test:e2e:full`)

## Code Quality

- Duck-typing → `instanceof` type checks
- Remove unnecessary `return await`, unused fields, duplicated code
- `as any` → type-safe mock patterns
- Unify null checks (`== null`)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/uppinote20/obsidian-auto-note-importer/compare/0.4.0...0.5.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.4.0 (2025-09-21)

### ✨ New Features

- **Formula Field Support**
  - Added support for Airtable Formula fields in filename and subfolder selection. Formula fields are now validated for filename compatibility with enhanced error handling and fallback mechanisms.

### 🔧 Improvements

- **Enhanced Field Validation**
  - Implemented field-type-specific validation logic: Formula fields receive strict validation while text, select, and number fields use basic sanitization for better compatibility.
  
- **Improved Error Handling**
  - Added robust fallback mechanisms when field validation fails, ensuring notes are always imported with safe filenames using record IDs as backup.

### ⚙️ Maintenance

- **Code Quality**
  - Resolved Claude Code review feedback regarding validation scope and field type differentiation.
  - Enhanced type safety and error handling throughout the validation pipeline.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.3.0 (2025-09-15)

### ✨ New Features

- **Subfolder Organization**
  - Automatically organize imported notes into subfolders based on Airtable field values. Support for nested structures like "Category/Subcategory".
  
- **Smart Field Selection UI**
  - Enhanced dropdown menus that only show safe field types (Text, Select, Number) for filename and subfolder selection. Unsupported fields are hidden with summary indicator.

- **Mobile Compatibility**
  - Plugin now works on mobile devices with full functionality across all Obsidian platforms.

### 🔧 Improvements

- **Enhanced Documentation**
  - Comprehensive field type reference and new template examples with advanced features.
  
- **Performance Optimizations**
  - API response caching, debounced UI updates, and recursive folder scanning with depth limits.

### ⚙️ Maintenance

- **Code Improvements**
  - Safer filename handling, enhanced error reporting, and improved template processing.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.2.4 (2025-09-05)

### ✨ New Features

- **Sync Progress Indicator**
  - A notice now appears in Obsidian showing the real-time progress of the note synchronization process (e.g., "Syncing 10/100..."). This provides better feedback, especially when importing a large number of notes.

### ⚙️ Maintenance

- **Dependency Updates**
  - Updated key development dependencies to their latest versions to improve stability and security.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.2.3 (2025-08-31)

### 🛠️ Template Processing Improvements

- **Enhanced Multi-line Text Handling in Templates**
	Improved how multi-line text values are processed in templates to ensure proper formatting:
	- **Frontmatter**: Multi-line values in YAML frontmatter now maintain proper indentation for valid YAML block scalars
	- **Body Content**: Multi-line text in note body preserves original formatting without unwanted indentation

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.2.2 (2025-05-27)

### 📝 Minor Fixes

- **Sentence Case Corrections**  
    Fixed command names, notices, and error messages in the UI that were not properly using **sentence case**

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.2.1 (2025-05-26)

### 🧼 UI & UX Polish

- **Followed Obsidian Community Guidelines**
    
    - Removed top-level headings (e.g., `<h2>`) from the settings tab.
        
    - Reworded all UI labels and descriptions to use **sentence case**, as required by Obsidian's plugin style guide.
        
    - Removed "Obsidian" from plugin metadata to comply with naming restrictions for third-party plugins.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.2.0 (2025-05-02) ### 🚀 New Features - **Configurable Primary & Filename Fields** You now have more control over how notes are identified and named: - **Primary Field**: Specify the Airtable field to use as a unique identifier. This prevents duplicates when "Allow Overwrite" is disabled. _Defaults to the first field in your Airtable view if left empty._ - **Filename Field Name**: Choose the Airtable field to use for the note's filename. _Defaults to `title`. Falls back to the Primary Field, then the Airtable record ID._ - **Advanced Template Parsing with Dot Notation** Template placeholders now support accessing nested values in complex Airtable fields using dot notation: _e.g., `{{Attachment.0.url}}`, `{{Created By.name}}`, `{{Linked Field.0}}`_ - **Airtable Pagination Support** Fetches all records across multiple pages (beyond Airtable's 100-record limit).
### ✨ Improvements - **Extended Airtable Field Type Support** The plugin now handles more Airtable field types—including Lookup, Rollup, Attachments, and User objects—making templates more expressive and robust. - **Enhanced Settings UI** - Airtable Personal Access Token input is now masked for better security. - **Automatic `primaryField` Injection in Frontmatter** To ensure reliable duplicate checking (when "Allow Overwrite" is off), the plugin now automatically adds a `primaryField: ` to the YAML frontmatter if it's missing. If no frontmatter block exists, a minimal one is created containing only the `primaryField`. - **Filename Sanitization** Filenames derived from Airtable fields are now automatically cleaned to remove invalid characters (`/ \ : * ? " < > | '`), preventing errors when saving notes. - **Safer Default Note Formatting** When no custom template is used: - The default note includes standard YAML frontmatter with fields like `videoId`, `tags`, `description`, etc. - Values are inserted using `formatYamlValue`, which ensures safer quoting and escaping to avoid YAML parsing issues. - **Documentation Enhancements** - The `README.md` now includes detailed instructions for using dot notation, handling various Airtable field types, and an improved example workflow. - A complete example template file (`template-example.md`) shows how to format notes with complex nested fields.
### 🔧 Internal Changes - Major updates to `main.ts`, `fetcher.ts`, `settings.ts`, and `note-builder.ts`. - Introduced `utils.ts` for reusable logic (`getNestedValue`, `sanitizeFileName`, `formatYamlValue`). - Modularized code for better maintainability and readability. - Updated `README.md` and `examples/template-example.md` to reflect new functionality.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.1.3 (2025-04-29)

### 🛠️ Fixes
- Replaced unsafe type casting with proper type guards (`instanceof`) for `TFolder` and `TFile` in settings management.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v0.1.2 (2025-04-28)

### ✨ Changes
- Removed the word **"Obsidian"** from `manifest.json`, `package.json`, `package-lock.json` and README descriptions to comply with community plugin submission guidelines.

releases.author: uppinote20
releases.description:
## v0.1.1 (2025-04-28)

### 🌟 First Public Release
- Automatically import Markdown notes from Airtable into your Obsidian vault.
- Supports both manual sync (`Sync Notes Now`) and scheduled auto-sync (interval-based).
- Customizable destination folder, template file, and overwrite options.
- Includes YAML frontmatter and a default note template.
- If the `title` field is missing, the plugin automatically uses the primary field as the filename.

### 🛠️ Fixes & Improvements
- Changed bundle output from `dist/` to root (`main.js`) for simpler CI releases.
- Resolved `.gitignore` conflicts by removing the `dist/` directory structure.
